ddls:
CREATE TABLE "network_traffic" (
  "packet_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each packet */,
  "highest_layer" TEXT /* Highest layer protocol used in the packet */,
  "transport_layer" TEXT /* Transport layer protocol used (e.g., TCP, UDP) */,
  "source_ip" TEXT /* Source IP address of the packet */,
  "dest_ip" TEXT /* Destination IP address of the packet */,
  "source_port" INTEGER /* Source port number */,
  "dest_port" INTEGER /* Destination port number */,
  "packet_length" INTEGER /* Length of the packet in bytes */,
  "packets_per_time" REAL /* Packets captured per time interval */,
  "target" INTEGER /* Target identifier for analysis purposes */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the packet was captured */,
  "protocol_id" INTEGER /* ID of the protocol used */,
  "device_id_source" INTEGER /* ID of the source device */,
  "device_id_dest" INTEGER /* ID of the destination device */,
  "segment_id_source" INTEGER /* ID of the source network segment */,
  "segment_id_dest" INTEGER /* ID of the destination network segment */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who captured or reviewed the packet */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the packet */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("packet_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_traffic_dest_ip FOREIGN KEY ("dest_ip") REFERENCES ip_addresses ("ip_address"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_traffic_dest_port FOREIGN KEY ("dest_port") REFERENCES ports ("port_number"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_traffic_segment_id_dest FOREIGN KEY ("segment_id_dest") REFERENCES network_segments ("segment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_traffic_protocol_id FOREIGN KEY ("protocol_id") REFERENCES protocols ("protocol_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_traffic_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_traffic_device_id_dest FOREIGN KEY ("device_id_dest") REFERENCES network_devices ("device_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_traffic_source_ip FOREIGN KEY ("source_ip") REFERENCES ip_addresses ("ip_address"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_traffic_source_port FOREIGN KEY ("source_port") REFERENCES ports ("port_number"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_traffic_highest_layer FOREIGN KEY ("highest_layer") REFERENCES protocols ("protocol_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_traffic_transport_layer FOREIGN KEY ("transport_layer") REFERENCES protocols ("protocol_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_traffic_device_id_source FOREIGN KEY ("device_id_source") REFERENCES network_devices ("device_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_network_traffic_segment_id_source FOREIGN KEY ("segment_id_source") REFERENCES network_segments ("segment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "protocols" (
  "protocol_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the protocol */,
  "protocol_name" TEXT /* Name of the protocol (e.g., TCP, UDP, BOOTP) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the protocol */,
  "port_range" TEXT /* Range of ports commonly used by the protocol */,
  "standard" INTEGER /* Indicates if the protocol is a standard or proprietary protocol */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("protocol_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "ip_addresses" (
  "ip_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the IP address */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address in standard notation */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the device using the IP address */,
  "description" TEXT /* Any additional information about the IP */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device using the IP address */,
  "segment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the network segment the IP address belongs to */,
  "last_seen" TEXT /* Last time the IP address was active */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the IP address (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("ip_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ip_addresses_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES network_devices ("device_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ip_addresses_segment_id FOREIGN KEY ("segment_id") REFERENCES network_segments ("segment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "ports" (
  "port_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the port */,
  "port_number" INTEGER /* Port number */,
  "service_name" TEXT /* Name of the service using this port (e.g., HTTP) */,
  "protocol" TEXT /* Protocol associated with the port (e.g., TCP, UDP) */,
  "common_services" TEXT /* Common services that use this port */,
  "default_protocol" TEXT /* Default protocol for the port */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("port_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the individual */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user within the system (e.g., Admin, Analyst) */,
  "full_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last time the user logged in */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the access log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user performing the action */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the action occurred */,
  "action" TEXT /* Type of action performed (e.g., view, modify) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the user accessed the system */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device used to access the system */,
  "duration" REAL /* Duration of the user's session */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES network_devices ("device_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the alert */,
  "packet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the packet that triggered the alert */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert generated (e.g., suspicious traffic) */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the alert was generated */,
  "resolved" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the alert has been resolved */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity level of the alert */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the alert */,
  "assigned_to" INTEGER /* ID of the user assigned to resolve the alert */,
  "resolution_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the alert was resolved */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_assigned_to FOREIGN KEY ("assigned_to") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_packet_id FOREIGN KEY ("packet_id") REFERENCES network_traffic ("packet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "traffic_analysis" (
  "analysis_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the analysis result */,
  "packet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the packet analyzed */,
  "analysis_result" TEXT /* Result of the traffic analysis (e.g., normal, suspicious) */,
  "analysis_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the analysis was performed */,
  "analysis_tool" TEXT /* Tool used for the analysis */,
  "confidence_score" REAL /* Confidence score for the analysis result */,
  "reviewed_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who reviewed the analysis */,
  "review_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the analysis was reviewed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("analysis_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_traffic_analysis_reviewed_by FOREIGN KEY ("reviewed_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_traffic_analysis_packet_id FOREIGN KEY ("packet_id") REFERENCES network_traffic ("packet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "packet_statistics" (
  "stat_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the statistic entry */,
  "total_packets" INTEGER /* Total number of packets captured in the time frame */,
  "total_bytes" INTEGER /* Total number of bytes transferred in the time frame */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the statistics were calculated */,
  "average_packet_size" REAL /* Average size of packets in the time frame */,
  "max_packet_size" INTEGER /* Maximum size of packets in the time frame */,
  "min_packet_size" INTEGER /* Minimum size of packets in the time frame */,
  "segment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the network segment for which the statistics are calculated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("stat_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_packet_statistics_segment_id FOREIGN KEY ("segment_id") REFERENCES network_segments ("segment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "network_devices" (
  "device_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the device */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the device */,
  "mac_address" TEXT /* MAC address of the device */,
  "device_type" TEXT /* Type of device (e.g., server, router, workstation) */,
  "manufacturer" TEXT /* Manufacturer of the device */,
  "model" TEXT /* Model of the device */,
  "os" TEXT /* Operating system of the device */,
  "last_seen" TEXT /* Last time the device was active on the network */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the device (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "network_segments" (
  "segment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the network segment */,
  "segment_name" TEXT /* Name of the network segment */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the segment */,
  "subnet" TEXT /* Subnet associated with the segment */,
  "gateway" TEXT /* Gateway IP address for the segment */,
  "dns_server" TEXT /* DNS server IP address for the segment */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the segment (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("segment_id")
)
